---
title: "Hovmoller"
output: html_document
---

# Libraries

```{r}

# Libraries
library(terra)
library(tidyverse)

```

# Hovmoller Diagram

## Function
```{r}

## Slice Processing Function
process_hov_slice <- function(file_path) {
  # 1. Grab the exact NDVI layer
  r_ndvi <- sds(file_path)[1]
  
  # 2. Apply the scale factor
  r_scaled <- r_ndvi * 0.00000001
  
  # 3. Extract as a true 1200x1200 2D grid
  ndvi_grid <- as.matrix(r_scaled, wide = TRUE)
  
  # 4. Collapse the Longitude (Mean of each row)
  lat_means <- rowMeans(ndvi_grid, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # 5. Get Y coordinates
  y_coords <- yFromRow(r_scaled, 1:nrow(r_scaled))
  
  # 6. Extract Date (YYYY.MM)
  date_match <- str_extract(file_path, "\\d{4}\\.\\d{2}")
  
  # 7. Create the Data Frame
  tibble(
    y = y_coords,
    date = as.Date(paste0(date_match, ".01"), format = "%Y.%m.%d"),
    ndvi = lat_means
  ) %>% 
    filter(!is.nan(ndvi))
}

```

## Senegal

```{r}
# Files
files <- list.files("Data/Vegetation.h16v07/", pattern="\\.hdf$", full.names=TRUE)

# Map function to all files and combine them into one table
hov_data_final <- lapply(files, process_hov_slice) %>% 
  bind_rows()

ggplot(hov_data_final, aes(x = date, y = y, fill = ndvi)) +
  geom_tile() +
  stat_contour(aes(z = ndvi), breaks = 0.2, color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) + # Black for better contrast against tan/green
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("tan", "khaki", "yellowgreen", "forestgreen", "darkgreen"),
    values = scales::rescale(c(-0.1, 0.15, 0.3, 0.6, 1)),
    name = "NDVI"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Hovmöller Diagram: Senegal Vegetation",
    x = "Timeline",
    y = "Latitude (Native Meters)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(
      legend.position = "right", 
      legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0))

ggplot(hov_data_final, aes(x = date, y = y, fill = ndvi)) +
  geom_tile() +
  stat_contour(aes(z = ndvi), breaks = 0.2, color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) + 
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("tan", "khaki", "yellowgreen", "forestgreen", "darkgreen"),
    values = scales::rescale(c(-0.1, 0.15, 0.3, 0.6, 1)),
    name = "NDVI"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "1 year",   # Change to "3 months" or "6 months" if it's too crowded
    date_labels = "%Y-%m",     # Formats as 2000-01, 2000-02, etc.
    expand = c(0, 0)           # Removes the empty white padding on the edges
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Hovmöller Diagram: Sahel Vegetation Dynamics",
    subtitle = "Black line tracks the 0.2 NDVI 'Desert Edge' boundary",
    x = "Timeline",
    y = "Latitude (MODIS Sinusoidal Meters)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

```

```{r}

# 1. Create a new column that tags each row as "Before 2007" or "2007 and After"
hov_data_split <- hov_data_final %>%
  mutate(
    period = if_else(date < as.Date("2007-01-01"), "Pre-2007", "2007-Present")
  )

# 2. Plot with facet_wrap
ggplot(hov_data_split, aes(x = date, y = y, fill = ndvi)) +
  geom_tile() +
  stat_contour(aes(z = ndvi), breaks = 0.2, color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) + 
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("tan", "khaki", "yellowgreen", "forestgreen", "darkgreen"),
    values = scales::rescale(c(-0.1, 0.15, 0.3, 0.6, 1)),
    name = "NDVI"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y-%m", expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(
    title = "Hovmöller Diagram: Sahel Vegetation Dynamics",
    x = "Timeline",
    y = "Latitude (MODIS Sinusoidal Meters)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  facet_wrap(~ period, scales = "free_x", ncol = 1)

```


## Chad

```{r}

```
